Pocketcard 5–1. You’re the Spokesperson—What You Need to Know
Prepare to Answer These Questions:
• Are my family and I safe?
• What can I do to protect myself
and my family?
Build Trust and Credibility by Expressing:
• Who is in charge here?
• Empathy and caring.
• What can we expect?
• Competence and expertise.
• Why did this happen?
• Honesty and openness.
• Were you forewarned?
• Commitment and dedication.
• Why wasn’t this prevented?
Top Tips:
• What else can go wrong?
• Don’t over-reassure.
• When did you begin working on this?
• Acknowledge uncertainty.
• What does this information mean?
• Express wishes (“I wish I had answers”).
• Explain the process in place to find Stay on Message:
answers. • “What’s important is to remember…”
• Acknowledge people’s fear. • “I can’t answer that question,
• Give people things to do. but I can tell you…”
• Ask more of people (share risk). • “Before I forget, I want to tell
your viewers…”
As a Spokesperson:
• Know your organization’s policies. • “Let me put that in perspective…”
• Stay within the scope of responsibilities.
CONSISTENT MESSAGES ARE VITAL
• Tell the truth. Be transparent.
• Embody your agency’s identify.
BE FIRST. BE RIGHT. BE CREDIBLE.
CERC: Spokesperson 22
